role_summary: SNCC field secretary and Tougaloo College student activist in the early 1960s Mississippi voter-registration project; co-subject of a joint oral history with her sister Joyce Ann Ladner (catalog entry 75).
ai_reading: Dorie Ann Ladner shares the embedding signature of the joint oral history at entry 75 (catalog slug 'ladners-joint-interview'); see that catalog page for the substrate-derived neighbor cluster and axis observations the embedding produces for the combined transcript. Individual cosines for each named subject of a joint interview are not separable from the precomputed substrate, since the LoC transcript was recorded as a single audio file with multiple voices interleaved.
biographical_paragraph: Dorie Ann Ladner is one of the named subjects of the joint Civil Rights History Project oral history at entry 75 (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. This catalog page exists as a per-person index for Dorie Ann Ladner; the substrate signature lives on the parent joint-interview page (see the AI's reading above) since the LoC transcript treats the joint conversation as a single recording. Dorie Ann Ladner's Civil Rights History Project oral history is cross-referenced against the Library of Congress published transcript.

sources.title: Joint oral history at entry 75
sources.publisher: Civil Rights History Project, Library of Congress (AFC 2010/039)
